body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Part 3 Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut non-refundable tax credits.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Part 3 calculates territorial non-refundable tax credits for a multiple-jurisdiction return. For Yukon, the Part 3 table uses federal return line inputs for line 30000 basic personal amount, line 30100 age amount, line 30300 spouse or common-law partner amount, line 30425 Canada caregiver amount for spouse or dependant, line 30400 eligible dependant amount, line 30450 infirm dependants age 18 or older, line 30500 caregiver amount, line 31260 Canada employment amount, line 31300 adoption expenses, line 31600 disability amount for self, and line 31800 disability amount transferred from a dependant. Yukon Part 3 also includes CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, children's arts amount, pension income capped at $2,000, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, medical expenses, medical expenses for other dependants, and Schedule 9 lines. Yukon uses a low credit rate of 6.4% encoded as 0.064 and a Schedule 9 high-rate credit rate of 12.8% encoded as 0.128. The Yukon medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,834 from eligible medical expenses.

For the Northwest Territories, the form uses a basic personal amount of $17,842 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 5.9% encoded as 0.059, and a Schedule 9 line 14 high-rate credit rate of 14.05% encoded as 0.1405. The Northwest Territories age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $8,727, an income threshold of $45,522, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 and eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 use a base amount of $17,842. Northwest Territories Part 3 also includes infirm dependant amount, CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, pension income capped at $1,000, caregiver and disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, medical expenses, medical expenses for other dependants, and Schedule 9 lines. The Northwest Territories medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,834 from eligible medical expenses.

For Nunavut, the form uses a basic personal amount of $19,274 on line 58040, a low credit rate of 4% encoded as 0.04, and a Schedule 9 line 14 high-rate credit rate of 11.5% encoded as 0.115. The Nunavut age amount worksheet line 58080 uses a maximum of $12,303, an income threshold of $45,522, and a 15% reduction rate encoded as 0.15. The spouse amount worksheet line 58120 and eligible dependant worksheet line 58160 use a base amount of $19,274. Nunavut line 58230 is the number of young children less than 6 years of age multiplied by $1,200. Nunavut Part 3 also includes infirm dependant amount, CPP or QPP contributions, enhanced CPP or QPP contributions, employment insurance premiums, enhanced EI premiums, pension income capped at $2,000, caregiver and disability amounts, disability supports, Schedule S11 or S11MJ amounts, student designated amounts, Schedule S2MJ amounts, medical expenses, medical expenses for other dependants, and Schedule 9 lines. The Nunavut medical expense worksheet line ME subtracts the lesser of 3% of net income, encoded as 0.03, and $2,834 from eligible medical expenses.

For each territory in this Part 3 batch, the encoded subtotal adds the basic personal or return-line amount, age amount, spouse amount, eligible dependant amount, selected listed credits, capped pension amounts, and disability-related lines. The total non-refundable tax credit line D multiplies the subtotal and later Part 3 lines by the territory's low credit rate and adds Schedule 9 line 14 multiplied by the territory's high-rate credit rate. Per-dependant row tables for infirm dependants, caregiver amounts, disability transfers, and medical expenses for other dependants, plus detailed S2 and S11 transfer schedules and the Nunavut young-child detail chart, are not encoded in this focused module.
